sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Clicking on the left panel checkerboard had no impact. So clicking on left panel objects doesn't do anything. The only interactive objects are on the right panel.
Let me reconsider the problem. I've established:
1. Clicking red shape on right toggles red rectangle between [24,29]x[18,21] and [30,33]x[12,17]
2. Clicking blue shape on right swaps blue square and checkerboard between [20,23]x[18,21] and [34,37]x[8,11]
3. White borders on right panel disappear after first click
4. Clicking on left panel objects has no effect
5. The level is not complete in any of the configurations I've tried so far
I've tried all 4 combinations of red/blue clicks:
1. Initial: blue at [20,23], checkerboard at [34,37], red at [30,33]
2. Blue clicked: checkerboard at [20,23], blue at [34,37], red at [30,33]
3. Red clicked: blue at [20,23], checkerboard at [34,37], red at [24,29]
4. Both clicked: checkerboard at [20,23], blue at [34,37], red at [24,29]
None of these completed the level. So there must be more to the puzzle.
Let me think about what else I could try:
1. Maybe I need to click on the white border dots in the middle column?
2. Maybe I need to use UP/DOWN/LEFT/RIGHT to navigate somewhere?
3. Maybe I need to click on other parts of the right panel?
4. Maybe I need to click on the gray boxes with colored squares?
Let me try clicking on the white border dots in the middle column (the vertical line of white dots).

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Clicking on left panel objects had no impact. Let me try clicking on the white dots in the middle column
# The white dots are at column 32, rows 17-43 (approximately)
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 30, 'col': 32}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
